name: WLF_MESSAGES
description: This table will be used to store all error and warning messages that are thrown during validation of the state of the learning item.

columns.Name: MESSAGE_CODE
columns.Comments: The fusion message name/key which can be used to lookup the appropriate translated value of the message

columns.Name: MESSAGE_CATEGORY
columns.Comments: Category of the message such as whether it is related to validation, transcoding, integration, etc.

columns.Name: SOURCE_OBJECT_ID
columns.Comments: The unique id of the source object to which this message applies

columns.Name: SOURCE_OBJECT_TYPE
columns.Comments: The type of the source object that this message is applicable to

columns.Name: VALIDATION_STATUS
columns.Comments: The status of the validation - ORA_ACTIVE / ORA_RESOLVED / ORA_IGNORE

columns.Name: SEVERITY
columns.Comments: The severity of the message - 1 for Error, 2 for Warning, 3 for Info

columns.Name: VISIBILITY
columns.Comments: The visibility of the message - ORA_SHOW / ORA_HIDE

columns.Name: TRACE_ID
columns.Comments: An internal identifier that can be used by Support/Dev team to trace the source/cause of the error

columns.Name: LOG
columns.Comments: Text to provide more information about the error

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
